title: Frontend architecture: split App.tsx into focused hooks and panels
body:
Category: frontend architecture

Problem:
`frontend/src/App.tsx` owns bridge calls, task polling, schema forms, settings, recent execution persistence, and rendering. This makes future tool features harder to maintain and test.

Proposed work:
- Extract bridge access into a focused hook/module.
- Extract task monitoring and polling into its own hook/panel boundary.
- Extract command catalog filtering and payload draft handling.
- Keep user-facing behavior unchanged while making the code easier to extend.

Acceptance criteria:
- Existing frontend tests still pass on a supported Node version.
- App.tsx becomes primarily composition instead of owning all workflows.

title: Native bridge architecture: split Python invocation, task lifecycle, permissions, and events
body:
Category: native bridge architecture

Problem:
`UnrealEditorWebUIBridge.cpp` currently combines permission approval, Python registry invocation, task storage/lifecycle, ticker logic, and browser event dispatch.

Proposed work:
- Move Python registry execution helpers into a focused native helper.
- Move task lifecycle/store concerns into a focused helper or module.
- Keep permission approval and event dispatch easier to audit.
- Preserve existing bridge API shape.

Acceptance criteria:
- UE automation/build still passes on the UE runner.
- Web bridge method behavior remains compatible.

title: Type safety: enable stricter TypeScript checks incrementally
body:
Category: frontend type safety

Problem:
The frontend processes bridge JSON and command schemas, but TypeScript strictness is still limited.

Proposed work:
- Enable `strict` first.
- Evaluate `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` and `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` in follow-up commits.
- Replace loose assumptions around bridge response and schema field rendering with explicit narrowing.

Acceptance criteria:
- `npm run build` succeeds on supported Node.
- Runtime behavior stays unchanged.

title: Task runtime: turn cooperative demo into a real long-running job protocol
body:
Category: task runtime

Problem:
`demo.longRun` currently exercises C++ ticker progress but does not dispatch a real Python cooperative handler.

Proposed work:
- Define a small cooperative job protocol for progress, cancellation, and timeout checks.
- Route demo long-run through the same protocol used by future long-running tools.
- Document which work is safe on editor tick vs editor game thread.

Acceptance criteria:
- The demo reports progress through the existing task UI.
- Cancellation and timeout behavior remain visible to the frontend.

title: Result UX: add real asset table row actions
body:
Category: frontend UX

Problem:
The asset table previously exposed a placeholder Select action. It has been removed, but useful row actions are still missing.

Proposed work:
- Add supported row actions such as copy/open/select where safe.
- Render only actions that are available for the current result type.
- Add tests for visible actions and disabled/unavailable states.

Acceptance criteria:
- No placeholder actions are shown.
- At least one useful asset row action is wired end-to-end.

title: CI: add Node and Python version matrix coverage
body:
Category: CI and compatibility

Problem:
Local validation found Python 3.9 and Node version compatibility gaps that CI did not cover directly.

Proposed work:
- Run Python registry tests on 3.9 and 3.11.
- Run frontend checks on Node 20.19+ and 22.13+.
- Keep UE-specific validation in the self-hosted workflow.

Acceptance criteria:
- Hosted CI catches the supported local toolchain floor.
- Existing validation steps remain intact.

title: Tool manifest: move project/stage catalog out of hard-coded frontend state
body:
Category: configuration/productization

Problem:
`frontend/src/tool-manifest.ts` hard-codes starter projects and stages. Production teams will need project-specific catalogs without editing frontend source.

Proposed work:
- Introduce a checked-in JSON manifest or bridge-loaded settings source.
- Validate stored preferences against the loaded manifest.
- Preserve current starter defaults as fallback data.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tool catalog defaults still work offline.
- Project/stage data can be changed without editing React component code.

title: Workflow automation: document or script issue/PR creation flow
body:
Category: repository workflow

Problem:
Repo maintenance work benefits from issue-first branches and linked PRs, but local environments may not have GitHub CLI installed or authenticated.

Proposed work:
- Document supported options: GitHub CLI, Git credential manager token, or browser session.
- Add a small maintainer script if useful for creating issue batches from a JSON file.
- Avoid storing secrets in the repository.

Acceptance criteria:
- Maintainers can reproduce issue/PR creation without guessing credentials setup.
- No tokens or private credentials are committed.
